name: Swedish Spritz Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by Midwest Living, December 1994. Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a mixing bowl, beat the butter and sugar with an electric mixer until fluffy.
Add the egg yolk and almond extract; beat well.
Beat or stir in the flour. Force the unchilled dough through a cookie press onto ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, or until the edges are firm but not brown. Cool on wire racks.
Variations: For nutty cookies, stir in 1/2 cup finely ground nuts with the flour. Toast the nuts before grinding if desired. For chocolate cookies, reduce the flour to 2 1/4 cups and add an additional 1/4 cup granulated sugar and 1/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder (add cocoa before flour). Optionally, dip tops in icing, then sprinkle with chopped nuts or colored sugar, or use decorative toppings. For marbled dough, alternate about 1/4 cup of each color of dough in the press, or marble white dough by adding coloring and kneading a few times before pressing.
